commit.message:
Update backend/orchestrator/Dockerfile

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Reset game state when returning to main menu (#99)

* Initial plan

* feat: reset game state when returning to main menu

- Add ReturnToMainMenu_Event to GameEvents (and ClearAll)
- RunController subscribes/unsubscribes to the event; on trigger it
  unsubscribes itself and destroys its DontDestroyOnLoad GameObject so
  duplicate controllers cannot accumulate across runs
- PauseMenuUI: add OnMainMenuClicked() that resumes time, fires the
  event, then loads the MainMenu scene
- PauseMenuBootstrap: add "Main Menu" button to the pause panel
- GameOverUI: fire ReturnToMainMenu_Event before loading MainMenu so
  RunController is always cleaned up
- MainMenuControllerTests: add two tests covering the new event

Co-authored-by: edvinklaebo <4660058+edvinklaebo@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: remove stray TEST_INFRASTRUCTURE_REPORT.txt

Co-authored-by: edvinklaebo <4660058+edvinklaebo@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add singleton guard to PauseMenuBootstrap to prevent duplicate instances

PauseMenuBootstrap in MainMenu.unity has a DoNotDestoryOnLoad component on
its GameObject. Each time MainMenu reloads, a new PauseMenuBootstrap was
instantiated by the scene while the original persisted, causing Start() to
fire twice and create a second pause menu UI inside the canvas.

Added the same singleton guard pattern used by RunStatsTrackerBootstrap:
- Awake() destroys the duplicate GameObject if an instance already exists
- OnDestroy() clears the static reference so the guard resets if the real
  instance is ever destroyed

Co-authored-by: edvinklaebo <4660058+edvinklaebo@users.noreply.github.com>

* ad

Signed-off-by: Edvin <edvin.klaebo@gmail.com>

feat: SDK upgrade, config flicker fix, CLI status tooltip (#170)

* fix: eliminate setup screen flicker on configured extensions

Use cached/prefetched auth state for the preliminary configStatus message
instead of always sending hasAuth=false. This prevents the webview from
briefly flashing the welcome screen during 30s auth polls, config changes,
window focus events, and panel reopens.

Work: Blair added _lastKnownHasAuth field to ChatViewProvider to cache auth state
between async checks, eliminating UI flicker on welcome screen.

Add rich MarkdownString tooltip to the Forge status bar item that displays

This helps users identify which CLI binary the extension resolved — useful
for debugging SDK/CLI version mismatches.

- Add _buildStatusBarTooltip() on ChatViewProvider for unified tooltip

- Add MarkdownString mock to test helpers

Resolves SDK/CLI protocol version mismatch. The CLI binary updated to
v1.0.2 (protocol v3) but SDK 0.1.26 only spoke protocol v2.

- Bump @github/copilot-sdk from 0.1.26 to 0.1.32

commit.message:
Update backend/orchestrator/app/store/findings_store.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: zero-frame output bug and improve code coverage

Root cause: save_fuzzed_frame used CGBitmapContextCreate with read-only
locked CVPixelBuffer data, causing NULL return (needs writable backing).
Also, fuzz() discarded all mutated frames; main() wastefully re-opened
the video 1377 times per run to save frames that never materialized.

Fixes:
- Replace CGBitmapContextCreate with CGImageCreate+CGDataProvider
  (copies pixel data, works with read-only lock)
- Save frames inline inside fuzz() instead of redundant video re-open
- Add error logging to save_fuzzed_frame at every failure point
- Return int status from save_fuzzed_frame for frame count tracking
- Track totalFramesSaved counter through main loop

Coverage improvements:
- Remove dead code: log_memory_info, malloc_with_guard, free_with_guard
  (defined in runner but never called — duplicated in interposer)
- Remove malloc_zone_print from log_gpu_memory_info (caused massive log
  spam: 1377 calls/run, multi-minute ASAN hangs)
- Remove unused includes: malloc/malloc.h, sys/mman.h, duplicate stdio.h
- Remove unused page_align macro
- Lower synthetic frame save interval: every 100th → every 10th iteration
- Reduce log frequency: periodic instead of every-iteration logging

Net: -190 lines dead code, +123 lines working frame save path.
Expected improvement: coverage from 53.65% → ~70%+ lines.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: stub large child sets, clean up discovery errors, add pkg manager actions

- discovery: stub nodes when parent has >50 subcommands (aws: 4.5s vs 125s)
- models: add Node.DiscoveryErr and Node.Stub fields (separate from Description)
- render: show '(…)' for stubs, '(?)' for discovery errors instead of verbose messages
- actions: add release-packages.yml for Homebrew/WinGet/Chocolatey/Scoop/AUR/Snap
- packaging: add template files for Chocolatey (.nuspec), AUR (PKGBUILD),
  Scoop (treemand.json), Snap (snapcraft.yaml)

Each pkg manager job is gated on its secret being present, so nothing
publishes until the maintainer opts in by adding credentials.

commit.message:
Remove parallel execution for v0.2 simplification (#36)

* Enable per-mutation temp file isolation for true parallelism (#EV-13)

Replace direct file writes in Integration::RSpec with temp-dir + $LOAD_PATH
isolation so multiple workers can safely mutate the same file in parallel.
Simplify Pool#partition to round-robin since file grouping is no longer needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Delete Parallel::Pool and Parallel::Worker (EV-22)

Remove parallel execution module and its specs. Sequential fork-based
isolation remains for mutation execution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove --jobs, parallel execution, and simplify Runner (EV-23, EV-24)

- Remove -j/--jobs CLI flag and jobs config option
- Remove parallel/pool require from runner.rb and evilution.rb
- Simplify Runner#run_mutations to always run sequentially
- Remove parallel execution test context and jobs-related specs

308 specs pass, no rubocop offenses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update docs and fix alignment after parallel removal (EV-25)

- Remove --jobs from README options table, config example, and example command
- Add [Unreleased] section to CHANGELOG noting parallel removal
- Remove Parallel::Pool from architect.md module list
- Fix indentation in runner_spec.rb config fixtures

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address review comments: deprecation warnings and multi-mutation spec

- Add multi-mutation runner spec verifying isolator runs for each
  mutation and results preserve count
- Add deprecation warning when --jobs flag is used (no longer crashes)
- Add deprecation warning when jobs: appears in config file
- Both warnings advise removal and are tested

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix review: hide --jobs from help, fix multi-mutation spec returns

- Strip --jobs/-j from argv before OptionParser so it doesn't appear
  in --help output while still warning on use
- Fix multi-mutation spec to chain .and_return on the expectation
  to avoid nil results breaking the reporter
- Add spec verifying --jobs is absent from help output

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Harden --jobs deprecation: handle all flag forms safely

- Handle -j4 (attached short form) and --jobs=4 (attached long form)
- Only skip next arg if it looks like a value (not another flag)
- Add specs for -j4 and --jobs=4 forms

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Only consume integer value after --jobs, not file arguments

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Apply suggestions from code review

* Apply suggestion from @marinazzio

* Apply suggestion from @marinazzio

commit.message:
fix: resolve all 12 known vulnerabilities

Library updates:
- github.com/go-git/go-git/v5 v5.4.2 → v5.17.0
  Fixes GO-2024-2456, GO-2024-2466, GO-2025-3367, GO-2025-3368, GO-2026-4473
  (path traversal/RCE, DoS, argument injection, .idx/.pack integrity)
- github.com/cloudflare/circl v1.6.1 → v1.6.3
  Fixes GO-2026-4550 (transitive dep of go-git v5.17.0)

Go toolchain upgrade:
- go 1.24.0 → go 1.26.1 in go.mod
- CI go-version: '1.24.x' → '1.26.x' in cicd.yaml and release.yml
  Fixes GO-2026-4337, GO-2026-4340, GO-2026-4341 (crypto/tls, net/url)
  Fixes GO-2026-4599, GO-2026-4600 (crypto/x509)
  Fixes GO-2026-4601, GO-2026-4602 (net/url, os)

Result: govulncheck reports 0 vulnerabilities in called code.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: 3x ETL speedup — single-pass cryo + Erigon RPC tuning + larger batches

1. Single cryo pass: extract blocks+txs+logs in one RPC sweep instead
   of 3 sequential runs (adx_etl.py process_batch rewrite)
2. Erigon RPC tuning: --rpc.batch.concurrency=8, --rpc.batch.limit=200,
   --db.read.concurrency=32 for higher parallel throughput
3. Batch size 2000→10000, chunk size matched

Expected: ~105 blocks/sec → 300-1000 blocks/sec

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Cy v0.2 proposal (#39)

This PR introduces the next design iteration. There will be a forum post covering it. The fundamental changes are:

- Reliability and reordering moved to the session layer from the
transport layer.
- Broadcast gossips amended with faster unicast gossips that scale
better. This addresses the high arrival load issue of the initial
design. See `cy/README.md` for rationale.
- Dependency on DSDL types removed completely -- no more piggybacking on
heartbeat.
- Session layer introduces a fixed 24-byte header; the backward
compatibility with Cyphal/CAN v1.0 will be addressed in a follow-up PR
(easy).

This PR also introduces an extensive test suite.

CC @laktoosivaba

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: cy
repository.description: Cyphal v1.1 in C: robust decentralized pub/sub with optional reliability and service discovery in a just couple thousand lines of code.

reading comprehension. Only M21 needed a structural comparison table.

commit.message:
refactor: consolidate duplicated utility functions and constants (#1961)

Five `formatMs` implementations, duplicated status color constants,
identical `useQueryState("search")` snippets in 5 files, and
inconsistent clipboard-copy patterns — consolidated into shared
utilities.

### `formatMs` / `formatDuration` → `utils/formatDuration.ts`

- `formatDuration()` — compact badge format (`"142ms"`, `"2.5s"`)
- `formatMs()` — verbose stats format with null handling (`"1,234 ms"`,
`"n/a"`)
- `nanoToMs()` — nanosecond conversion from QueryProfilePanel

Removed local copies from `PipelineDetailPanel`, `PipelineListPanel`,
`NodeDetailPage`, `QueryProfilePanel`. `panelBadgeUtils.formatMs` now
delegates to `formatDuration`.

### Status color constants → `dataStreamsUtils.ts`

- Removed duplicate `STATUS_CHIP_COLORS`, `STREAM_STATUS_ORDER`,
`toFieldRows` from `DataStreamsPage.tsx`
- Exported `STREAM_STATUS_ORDER` from `dataStreamsUtils`; switched
inline lookup to `getStatusChipColor()`

### `useSearchParam()` hook

```ts
export function useSearchParam() {
  return useQueryState("search", parseAsString.withDefault("").withOptions({ history: "replace" }));
}
```

Replaced identical 4-line snippet in `IndicesPage`, `DataStreamsPage`,
`UsersPage`, `RolesPage`, `useDataStreamsPageState`.

### Clipboard copy → `useCopyFeedbackTimeout`

- **ApiKeysPage**: replaced manual `useRef` + `setTimeout` cleanup with
`useCopyFeedbackTimeout`
- **PipelineDetailPanel**: replaced raw
`navigator.clipboard.writeText()` with `copyToClipboard()` +
`useCopyFeedbackTimeout`, adding "Copied!" tooltip feedback

- Fixes elastic/ai-github-actions-playground#1948

---

managed](https://ela.st/github-ai-tools) by the [Update PR Body
workflow](https://github.com/elastic/ai-github-actions-playground/actions/runs/22802950324).

commit.message:
fix: auto-stop silence detection - use timestamp parsing instead of string prefix match

0.x timestamps (e.g. silence_start: 0.8), causing auto-stop to never
fire when the user spoke and stopped within the first second.

Now parses the actual float timestamp and only ignores the initial
ambient silence at t < 0.5s.

commit.message:
fix: use lowercase JSON property names for workspace dropdown

Wails serializes Go structs using encoding/json, which respects the
json struct tags (lowercase). The frontend was using w.ID/w.Name but
the serialized JSON has w.id/w.name, causing the workspace dropdown
to render 'undefined' values and save 'undefined' as the workspace ID.

Also adds a guard against saving 'undefined' as workspace value.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: 项目重构 — 前后端分离 (frontend/backend/test) (#44)

将 monorepo 重构为三个独立子项目：

## 变更概述
- **backend/**: Java/Spring Boot 后端，新增 SPA 路由控制器
- **frontend/**: React + TypeScript + Vite 前端（8 页面, 11 组件, 14 单元测试）
- **test/**: Python + pytest + Playwright E2E 测试（24 API + 49 浏览器）

## 详细变更
- 移动 video/, youtube/, pom.xml 到 backend/
- 初始化 Vite+React+TypeScript 前端项目
- 创建 Python 测试框架（pytest + Playwright）
- 删除旧 JS Playwright 和 Java E2E 测试
- CI 改为 4 个 job：后端测试、前端测试、API E2E、浏览器 E2E
- 更新 README 和 CHANGELOG

commit.message:
feat: modern view! macro with conditionals, loops, binding, and components

Major framework overhaul — Oxide v0.2:

view! macro enhancements:
- {if cond { <view> } else { <view> }} — reactive conditional rendering
- {for item in expr { <view> }} — reactive list rendering
- bind:value={signal} — two-way form input binding
- bind:checked={signal} — two-way checkbox binding
- class:name={bool} — conditional CSS class toggling
- <Component prop={val} /> — struct-based component system

Runtime additions:
- Component trait for declarative component composition
- provide_context() / use_context() for dependency injection
- Context system using TypeId-keyed thread-local HashMap

Showcase rewrite: 1216 → 882 lines (-28%) with same 16 demos,
now using declarative syntax instead of imperative DOM manipulation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(wallpaper): we-sync scans workshop + WE bundled defaultprojects

Refactored we-sync to scan multiple sources via sync_source_dir():

- WE defaultprojects - 20 bundled wallpapers
- WE myprojects - any user-created wallpapers

All sources handle preview.jpg and preview.gif equally.

commit.message:
Change separators to TAB/LF/CR escape sequences

Replace byte 1/2/3 separators with \t (9), \n (10), \r (13) which
are valid Novus escape sequences and have entries in _byte_to_char.

This avoids the ARM64 store8 buffer corruption bug entirely by using
string literals from the data section instead of runtime allocation.

- my_sep() returns "\t" (field separator)
- my_row_sep() returns "\n" (row separator)
- my_null_marker() returns "\r" (NULL marker)
- Updated byte_at comparisons: 2->10 (row), 3->13 (null)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(coc): add /skill-name slash-command autocomplete in EnqueueDialog prompt

Integrate the existing slash-command infrastructure (useSlashCommands hook,
SlashCommandMenu component, slash-command-parser) into the Enqueue AI Task
dialog's prompt textarea.

When the user types '/' in the prompt:
- A filtered autocomplete menu appears with available skills
- Arrow keys navigate, Tab/Enter/click selects a skill
- Escape dismisses the menu
- Selecting a skill updates both the prompt text and the skill dropdown
- On submit, /skill-name tokens are parsed from the prompt and used as
  the effective skill (dropdown selection takes priority if set)

Placeholder text updated to hint at the feature: 'Type / for skills'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
